response.justification: The candidate is a skilled full-stack and systems developer whose primary expertise lies in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not C++. C++ appears only in a single historical instrumentation role (NI LabWindows/CVI) and is absent from the skills section entirely; there is no evidence of sustained Windows-platform C++ development approaching the required 5–10 year threshold. The candidate also has no fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge — the one finance-adjacent role involved TypeScript/GraphQL pricing tooling, not capital-markets systems. Neither the mandatory technical requirement nor the domain requirement is met.
strengths: Demonstrated polyglot engineering ability across many languages and platforms shows broad adaptability
One role involved low-latency, high-throughput micro-services infrastructure handling millions of requests per second, indicating performance-oriented systems thinking
Active CI/CD and DevOps experience (GitLab, Docker, Artifactory, trunk-based development) aligns with optional desirable skills
concerns: C++ is not listed in the skills section and surfaces only in one older instrumentation role; no evidence of the required 5–10 years of hands-on Windows-platform C++ development
No fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets domain knowledge anywhere in the resume
Core stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang/web, which is fundamentally misaligned with the role's mandatory C++/SQL Server/Oracle on Windows profile

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ depth and platform alignment
No capital markets or fixed income domain exposure
Low-latency systems and broad engineering experience
